channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Added Retry Logic to My SQLite Failure Library. Here's the Exponential Backoff Pattern That Works.
item.description: Hook My SQLite failure library started throwing "database is locked" errors under concurrent load. Multiple agents writing failures simultaneously caused write contention. I needed retry logic —...

item.title: Blade Runner 2099’s moody dystopia streams on Amazon in November
item.description: After teasing the series with some first-look images yesterday, Amazon is finally properly unveiling its Blade Runner streaming series. Called Blade Runner 2099, the show hits Prime Video on...

item.title: Why comment-based UGC consent tracking fails silently — and the append-only ledger that fixes it
item.description: Disclosure: I'm the founder of CollectSocials. This is an engineering write-up of a data-modeling problem I ran into while evaluating UGC-rights tools, including building our own. I've kept it to...

item.title: Claude Opus 5 is Live on Agent Platform 🚀
item.description: Claude 5 Opus is officially available in the Model Garden on Agent Platform! Built for highly complex, long-running agentic workflows, Claude 5 Opus brings advanced capabilities in coding, vision,...

item.title: React Component Lifecycle Methods: Explained with an Interactive Diagram
item.description: A detailed, beginner-friendly guide to React class component lifecycle methods, render vs commit phases, cleanup, error boundaries, deprecated methods, and Hook equivalents. If you have worked...

item.title: You can’t ignore Google Zero anymore
item.description: The web and Google once had a deal: Google collects data and indexes webpages and in exchange sends oceans of traffic to websites. The deal wasn't perfect and certainly made Google more money than...

item.title: Open Serverless v4 is here
item.description: We're long time users of Serverless Framework since v2. When v3 came out, we were able to quickly switch and all was good. Then came v4 and things changed, especially since a new licensing and...

item.title: [Day 1]-I Learned the Foundation of HTML
item.description: Hello everyone! This marks the Day 1 of my learning journey of becoming Full-Stack Web Developer, which is exciting because this is my first day of documenting my learning progress. In the...

item.title: Unlimited-OCR: Parsing a 40-Page PDF in One Pass Without Your GPU Melting
item.description: If you have ever built a document-parsing pipeline, you know the ritual. Split the PDF into pages. Run each page through the OCR model. Stitch the outputs back together. Then write a pile of glue...

item.title: Mouse over WiFi
item.description: Today I found that I needed to connect a USB mouse to my laptop — and both USB-C ports were occupied, one by the monitor, one by power. Turns out it's not a huge deal on Linux if you have another...

item.title: Types of Functions in JavaScript — Differences Explained with Examples
item.description: Types of Functions in JavaScript — Differences Explained JavaScript has several ways to define functions. They look similar, but they differ in hoisting, this, arguments, and whether they can be...

item.title: Why Sequential Reads Beat Random Reads in Postgres at Scale
item.description: We had a large table - hundreds of gigabytes - that needed to be fully exported and processed in batches. The pipeline fetched rows from Postgres in batches of 2000, processed them, and pushed...

item.title: The quest to keep organs alive outside the body
item.description: This week, I covered a fascinating effort to preserve organs outside the body. There’s a huge shortage of donor organs, and one of the main reasons is time—they survive only a matter of hours...

item.title: Anthropic launches Opus 5
item.description: Opus 5 will be both cheaper and less restrictive than Fable, likely making it preferable in most use cases

item.title: Anthropic releases Opus 5 with ‘close’ to Fable 5’s capabilities
item.description: Weeks after Anthropic's latest toe-to-toe with the US government, and days after an OpenAI security incident that dominated tech industry discussions, Anthropic on Thursday released its newest...

item.title: Meta is making its AI chatbot more like an assistant
item.description: Meta is upgrading its AI chatbot with new productivity features in a bid to compete with rivals like Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude. The update will allow Meta AI to tap into your calendar to help...

item.title: As US weighs response to Chinese AI, industry urges against broad open-weight restrictions
item.description: AI companies, including Nvidia and Mistral, urge policymakers to avoid broad restrictions on open-weight AI models as Washington debates responses to Chinese AI and alleged model distillation.

item.title: Tesla’s car door defect could lead to tougher rules for everyone
item.description: Tesla's electronic door handles that have been linked to several deaths could lead to tougher safety rules for the entire auto industry. In a notice published today, the National Highway Traffic...

item.title: Bluesky’s AI assistant Attie expands into an open social research tool
item.description: Users can now ask Attie questions about news, trends, and conversations on Bluesky and other apps on the AT Protocol.

item.title: BlueNoroff Zoom Phishing Kit Profiles Crypto Wallets Before Malware Delivery
item.description: The North Korean threat actors behind the ClickFix-style campaigns that employ typosquatted Zoom and Microsoft Teams domains have been found to operate an active phishing kit to impersonate the...

item.title: One of the best 2-in-1 Chromebooks is almost $200 off for today only
item.description: Most Chromebooks ship with only 8GB of RAM, which is enough for some people, but certainly not enough for me. Multitaskers who want more memory in their next Chromebook should check out Best Buy,...

item.title: Midjourney acquired the astrology app Co-Star
item.description: The AI lab Midjourney continues to expand its purview beyond image and video generation.

item.title: Boox is taking on Xteink with its own tiny e-reader
item.description: Boox's new Tile lineup will take on Xteink's popular e-readers that are small enough to stick on the back of an iPhone with MagSafe. As spotted by Good E-Reader, the first one is the Picco, which...

item.title: Silicon Valley Is Completely Divided Over Chinese AI
item.description: The AI “startups” worth billions of dollars are raising alarm bells about Chinese AI. The smaller players have a totally different take.

item.title: The tech-broification of American science has officially begun
item.description: The Trump administration unveiled the first "Genesis Mission" grants on Thursday, directing $5 billion toward hundreds of AI-driven science projects in an effort the White House has described as...

item.title: Certighost Exploit Lets Low-Privileged Active Directory Users Impersonate a Domain Controller
item.description: Researchers H0j3n and Aniq Fakhrul published a working exploit on July 24 that lets a low-privileged Active Directory user obtain a certificate for a Domain Controller and authenticate as that...

item.title: ‘AI communism’, rogue models, and the why Kimi K3 spooked Wall Street
item.description: Chinese AI lab Moonshot’s open model Kimi went viral this week for reasons that had less to do with the model itself and more to do with how the U.S. AI industry reacted to it. Meanwhile, an...

item.title: What I track in a day
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life....

item.title: OpenAI’s new voice mode makes it to the ChatGPT desktop app
item.description: ChatGPT Voice on desktop can work with both ChatGPT Work and Codex to complete tasks and control agents.

item.title: The Download: an organ transplant breakthrough, and homegrown Chinese chips
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Supercooled kidneys have been transplanted into pigs in a...
